data.content: To become a Kravix Rider, you must download the Rider App, upload your Aadhar Card, Driving License, and Vehicle RC. A background check is performed. Verification usually takes 24 hours. Once verified, you will receive the Kravix Rider Bag and T-shirt.

Rider base earnings are calculated per delivery. It includes a base pickup fee (₹15), a drop-off fee (₹15), and a per-KM distance fee (₹5/km). Waiting time at the restaurant beyond 15 minutes adds ₹2 per minute.

Riders can earn Weekly Incentives by completing milestones. E.g., Completing 50 orders in a week grants a ₹500 bonus. Rainy day bonuses (₹15 extra per order) and late-night bonuses (₹20 extra between 11 PM and 2 AM) apply automatically.

Upon accepting an order, navigate to the restaurant. Swipe 'Arrived' when within 100 meters. Check the order number with the seller. Verify the contents, pack them in the Kravix thermal bag, and swipe 'Picked Up' to start the delivery phase.

Navigate to the customer's drop location using the in-app map. Upon reaching, politely ask for the 4-digit Delivery OTP provided in the customer's app. Enter the OTP in your rider app to mark the order as 'Delivered'. Never handover food without the OTP.

Penalties are levied for order cancellation after acceptance (₹50 penalty), rude behavior reported by customers, or food tampering. A high cancellation rate or severe policy violation will result in a temporary or permanent ID block.

Rider payouts are processed every Tuesday for the previous week's earnings (Monday-Sunday). The money is directly transferred to the registered bank account via NEFT/UPI. Real-time cashout is available for a ₹10 instant transfer fee.

Kravix offers flexible shifts (Free Login) and Fixed Shifts. Fixed Shift riders must log in during their booked slots (e.g., 12 PM - 4 PM) to maintain a 90% attendance rate, which makes them eligible for minimum guarantee pay.

If a rider faces issues (e.g., vehicle breakdown, accident, customer not reachable, restaurant closed), they can use the 'Rider Support / SOS' button in the app. The dispatch team will reassign the order or guide the rider accordingly.

All active Kravix riders are covered under a basic accidental insurance policy while on duty, covering hospitalization up to a set limit. Claims are filed through the Rider Support section with medical documents.

Riders must upload valid Vehicle RC (Registration Certificate) and Pollution Certificate (PUC) for verification. Vehicles that fail verification cannot be used to accept orders until documents are updated.

Rider Acceptance Rate measures the percentage of assigned orders accepted, and Completion Rate measures successfully delivered orders. Riders with low rates over a rolling 7-day window may receive fewer high-priority order assignments.

Riders are rated by customers (for delivery experience) and by restaurants (for pickup punctuality and behavior) after each order. Average ratings are visible to the rider and factor into weekly incentive eligibility.

The Rider App provides turn-by-turn GPS navigation to both the restaurant and customer location, integrated with live traffic. If GPS signal is weak, riders can manually enter the address or call the customer using the in-app masked number.

Besides OTP, riders may be asked to capture a Proof of Delivery photo for high-value or contactless orders where the customer requests 'leave at door'. This photo is stored and visible to support in case of disputes.

Riders can refer new riders using a unique referral code. Once the referred rider completes 30 deliveries within their first month, both the referrer and the new rider receive a one-time bonus credited to their earnings.

During heavy rain or extreme weather, riders can pause availability without it affecting their attendance streak. Riders who continue delivering during declared 'weather alerts' automatically qualify for the rainy day bonus.
